components.title: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
components.description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (MDE) Plan 2 deployed on all Windows 11 workstations and Windows Server 2022 systems. Provides next-generation antivirus (Defender AV), EDR (endpoint detection and response), threat and vulnerability management (TVM), attack surface reduction (ASR) rules, and controlled folder access. Managed via Microsoft Intune and Defender portal.
components.purpose: Protect endpoints from malware, exploits, and advanced threats; enable EDR capability.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.

implemented-requirements.description: Microsoft Defender Antivirus is configured in active mode with real-time protection, cloud-delivered protection (MAPS), and automatic sample submission enabled. Definitions update every 4 hours via Windows Update and Windows Defender Update. Scheduled full scan: Sundays 02:00. Quick scan: daily 06:00. Exclusions are minimized and documented; any AV exclusion requires CISO approval.

MDE EDR provides behavioral detection of advanced threats not caught by signature-based AV: process injection, credential harvesting, living-off-the-land binaries (LOLBins), and lateral movement. EDR telemetry is streamed to Microsoft Sentinel via the Defender XDR connector. Automated Investigation and Response (AIR) is enabled to automatically contain confirmed malware.

MDE Threat and Vulnerability Management (TVM) provides continuous vulnerability discovery without requiring separate scan credentials. TVM results are reviewed weekly by the security team; critical vulnerabilities (CVSSv3 ≥9.0) are escalated to the patch team within 24 hours. TVM exposure score is tracked as a KPI in the weekly security report.

MDE Attack Surface Reduction (ASR) rules are enabled in Block mode: block Office applications from creating child processes, block Office from injecting into other processes, block credential stealing from LSASS, block execution of potentially obfuscated scripts, block untrusted/unsigned processes from USB, and use advanced protection against ransomware. ASR audit mode is used for 30 days before block mode on new rules.

MDE Network Protection is enabled in block mode: prevents connections to known malicious IP addresses and domains (Microsoft threat intelligence). Web Content Filtering blocks categories: adult content, gambling, illegal activity, and anonymizer proxies. SmartScreen is enforced for all browsers managed by Intune.

MDE generates detailed forensic telemetry retained for 180 days in the Defender portal: process creation/termination (with command lines), network connections, file creation/modification, registry changes, and logon events. This data is available for threat hunting and incident investigation via Advanced Hunting (KQL).
